tipRules.tip: Hackney: 'If I can get 10 jig bites over 20 other bites on different bait, my chances of catching a bigger than average fish are higher. The jig produces bigger fish even if the difference is 3.5-pounders vs 2.5-pounders'

Hackney: 'The Hack Attack jig has a 27-degree line tie putting it almost at the center of the head. It goes in and out of cover easily and leaves nothing in the way of the hook point — no hook-up interference from the line tie'

Hackney: 'Use a heavy hook that won't flex. A hook that flexes will cut its way out of the fish's mouth. You need a strong shaft to withstand pressure in heavy cover — it will penetrate and go straight in'

knowledge.insight: Hackney: 'A 90- or 60-degree line tie covers up half the bite on your hook point. With a big weighted jig like 3/4 oz, the line tie can catch in the roof of their mouth instead of the hook point — feels hooked but the hook never penetrated'
